name: Hyperbola-1

overview.value: Hyperbola-1 (aka Shuangquxian-1, SQX-1) is a Chinese four-stage, all-solid-fuel rocket guided by liquid-fuel attitude control engines, 20.8 m tall and 1.4 m in diameter, weighing 31 t. It is manufactured by i-Space (Beijing Interstellar Glory Space Technology Co., Ltd.) and its current status is active.

base.reason: independent China-space-sector newsletter, single reference (registry draft, 2026-07-08)

notes: Added 2026-07-08 per Florian (verified during the backfill, previously skipped as off the phase-3 authorized list). Flight history from china-in-space.com's launch-by-launch account: the source states the 2025-07-29 mission was the eighth flight and narrates each prior flight's outcome individually (2019-07 success, 2021-02/2021-08/2022-05 failures, 2023-04/2023-12 successes, 2024-07 failure); the 4-success total is counted from those individually stated outcomes, not from a stated cumulative figure, hence provisional tier. vehicle_class, reusable, price left null: no fetched page literally stated them. LL2 not polled (shared rate limit); the weekly enrich-registry job will fill/confirm from LL2 at canonical tier.
